title: ArAnagnorisisStep
description: A11 (aristotelian-sketch-02). One staggered character-level recognition within an ArMythos, supplementary to the mythos's singular anagnorisis_event_id. Reached via ArMythos.anagnorisis_chain; not authored at encoding scope. `character_ref_id` is required — A11 keeps steps strictly character-level (audience-level recognition is sketch-01 A4 / A8 scope-out). `precipitates_main=True` declares that this step causes the mythos's main anagnorisis; A7.7 invariant 2 enforces that the step's τ_s strictly precedes the main anagnorisis event when substrate events are threaded. Step event_ids must appear in the enclosing mythos's central_event_ids (A7.7 invariant 1) and must not equal anagnorisis_event_id (A7.7 invariant 3). PFS13-AS1..AS4.

id.description: Stable identifier for this step (e.g., 'arstep_jocasta_realizes'). PFS13-AS1.

event_id.description: Substrate event id at which the recognition lands. Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2. Existence / central-event membership / non-equality with anagnorisis_event_id are A7.7 invariants enforced by the verifier. PFS13-AS2.

character_ref_id.description: Character who realizes — points at an ArCharacter id in the enclosing mythos's characters tuple. Required per A11 (anonymous step would be audience-level, sketch-02 rejects). Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2. PFS13-AS2.

precipitates_main.description: Does this step cause the mythos's main anagnorisis? When true, A7.7 invariant 2 enforces the step's τ_s strictly precedes the main anagnorisis event's τ_s (when substrate events are threaded). Python default is false; schema admits absence and false interchangeably. PFS13-AS3.

annotation.description: Optional author prose annotation. Python default is empty string; schema admits the empty string and absence interchangeably. PFS13-AS4.

step_kind.description: A14 (aristotelian-sketch-03). Closed enum classifying the chain step. {parallel} is a non-causal sibling recognition (Lady Macbeth's sleepwalking; Gloucester's blinding). {precipitating} causes the mythos's main anagnorisis (Jocasta in Oedipus). {staging} sets up the main anagnorisis by displaying evidence to the recognizer (Hamlet's Mousetrap). Empty string (Python default) means 'derive from precipitates_main + character identity' for back-compat; A7.11 carries the derivation rules. Unlike A10/A13's canonical-plus-open kind discipline, step_kind is closed at the schema layer — invalid non-empty values are rejected at shape time, matching peripeteia_anagnorisis_binding (PFS13-M13). PFS16-AS5.

anagnorisis_qualifier.description: A20 (aristotelian-sketch-06). Closed enum typing whether the recognition this step carries is {genuine}, {anti} (real but too late to alter outcome — Webster's Antonio recognizing Bosola as the mortal wound lands), or {partial} (an incomplete grasp — the Hamlet Claudius-prayer shape). Empty string (Python default) is read as genuine/unspecified; pre-sketch-06 chains validate unchanged. Orthogonal to step_kind — an anti recognition may be parallel, precipitating, or staging. A7.18 checks the vocabulary and notes anti/partial steps that also precipitate the main recognition. Closed at the schema layer like step_kind. AS06-1.
